_doc: Per-fixture CONTENT-ADDRESSED known-answer ledger (SIGNAL-ADAPTER-AND-FRONTIER Phase 7 — supersedes the B-lite manifestHash-only sidecar AND retires the verify-provenance buildHash fallback). Each fixture record is a self-contained known-answer triple: hash(input) + executed-code manifestHash -> hash(output). FIELDS — `bundle`: the producing app; `manifestHash`: SHA-256[0:12] of the UUID-independent projection of the producing bundle's __bundler/manifest at generation time (the EXECUTED-code identity, the SAME value verify-provenance.html GATE A holds the bundle to; PROVENANCE-NONDETERMINISM-2026-06-29 §1 made it deterministic); `inputHashes`: { committed-input-filename: SHA-256[0:16] of that file's RAW bytes } ({} for synthetic in-code fixtures); `outputHash`: SHA-256[0:16] of the committed fixture file's RAW bytes. A HISTORICAL record carries `historical:true` + `outputHash` ONLY — byte-PINNED, NOT code-gated (an immutable snapshot whose producing code has evolved, so it is not current-code-reproducible; code-gating it would assert a false reproducibility — the OxyDex_2026-06-17_2042 rule). verify-provenance.html GATE B (shared core ManifestGate.gateBEvaluate) reds a code-gated fixture the moment its bundle's CURRENT manifestHash, any committed INPUT, or the committed OUTPUT changes — NO buildHash anywhere (no runtime race), pure-static. The `fixtures` keys ARE the audited set (no separate legacy list). HOW TO ADD/UPDATE: re-run the fixture's app on its committed inputs and re-export (NEVER hand-edit a hash — that defeats the gate); then record the producing bundle's manifestHash (verify-provenance GATE A column) + sha256[0:16] of every committed input + the committed output (helpers ManifestGate.sha16 / gateBFiles / gateBEvaluate, byte-shared by the page + the Node sibling tests/verify-manifest.mjs). manifestHash is deterministic, so an EXPORT-INERT re-bundle of identical source moves NOTHING — re-record ONLY when the producing code actually changed the output.
_note_content_addressed_phase7: SIGNAL-ADAPTER-AND-FRONTIER-2026-06-23-BRIEF Phase 7 (content-addressed provenance) executed 2026-06-30. Each fixture record gained `inputHashes` (sha256[0:16] of every committed input file's raw bytes) + `outputHash` (sha256[0:16] of the committed fixture file's raw bytes), folding the known-answer triple hash(input)+manifestHash->hash(output) into ONE record (the brief's 'fold ... into one record per (fixture x bundle)'). The 2 historical Integrator fusions (integrator_fusion_2026-06-13/-11.json) were brought IN as `historical:true` byte-pinned records, retiring the verify-provenance LEGACY_FIXTURES buildHash-fallback list (the 3 pre-R1 PulseDex summaries + ppgdex_20260610 were DROPPED from the audit — PulseDex/PpgDex already carry code-gated fixtures, so no node lost coverage; those files remain in uploads/ as untracked samples). verify-provenance.html is now a PURE-STATIC gate (no iframe boot, no buildHash read); GATE B = ManifestGate.gateBEvaluate over the content hashes. buildHash is RETIRED as a provenance signal fleet-wide (still stamped into exports by the bundled ganglior-provenance.js as inert legacy metadata — NOT re-bundled away, which would churn every fixture for zero gate value; no gate reads it). TEST/LEDGER/DOC pass — ZERO app re-bundle (manifestHash unchanged on all 8; the brief's 'forces re-bundling all apps' cost estimate predated the deterministic-manifestHash pass that already moved the honest hash to a static computation). GATE A 8/8 + GATE B 15/15 green by construction.

_profileCoupledStripList.doc: SYNTH-TEXTURE-FOLLOWUPS-IV §1 (decided 2026-06-24, option b). These newMetrics fields are USER-PROFILE-coupled (derived from HRmax/HRrest = f(age,sex)), and the profile is NOT captured by provenance.inputs (which fingerprints only the signal CSV). A naive 're-run + re-export' under a different default profile moves them with no code/signal change (e.g. age 49 -> hrMax 174 vs age 50 -> hrMax 173). Rather than commit a *.profile.json the app must read, they are documented + strip-listed: GATE C (verify-provenance.html) excludes them from byte-diff, and FOLLOWUPS-III held them at canonical when regenerating OxyDex_2026-06-13_1056. They are signal-inert.

A faithful re-run+re-export was not achievable: its original user profile (incl. elevation/cpap, which materially shift SpO2 thresholds + apnea framing) was never recorded, and the live app runs on the operator's real localStorage profile, so a re-export could not reproduce the committed non-entropy fields without guessing + risking the operator's stored health data. Retired rather than regenerated; the 1056 fixture already provides current, sidecar-gated OxyDex provenance coverage. REGENERATE the input and this golden together whenever the export shape changes.
